rulings.Case Title: JAMES BONG ENTERPRISES, INC. VS. MARIELLEN BAKER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 16, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA GIRL, LLC, CALIFORNIA GIRL JEWELRY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to February 27, 2024 per stipulation and order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: BRETT MILLIER, AS SUCCESSOR CO-TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL VS. MARC DANA MERKER, AN INDIVIDUAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT FOR PARTITION BY SALE OF REAL PROPERTY, FOR APPOINTMENT OF PARTITION REFEREE, FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER, AND FOR OTHER RELIEF
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 16, 2024 line 2. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT FOR PARTITION BY SALE OF REAL PROPERTY, FOR APPOINTMENT OF PARTITION REFEREE, FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER, AND FOR OTHER RELIEF is continued to January 19, 2024 for Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief addressing: (1) the sufficiency of notice of the entry of December 20, 2023 order for January 8, 2023 hearing given the proof of service indicating that the December 20, 2023 order was electronically served on January 5, 2024. (2) Identify the allegation(s) in the Complaint by page and line number corresponding to Plaintiff's request in the proposed interlocutory judgment "to deduct the principal amount of $305,113.84, plus interest, from Defendant, Marc Dana Merker's 50% share of the net proceeds of sale." There shall be no argument included. (3) Identify the portion of the Points and Authorities in the moving papers by page and line number addressing the request in the proposed interlocutory judgment "to deduct the principal amount of $305,113.84, plus interest, from Defendant, Marc Dana Merker's 50% share of the net proceeds of sale." There shall be no argument included. (4) Identify evidence submitted with the moving papers by the declarant's name, paragraph number and exhibit number corresponding to Plaintiff's request in the proposed interlocutory judgment "to deduct the principal amount of $305,113.84, plus interest, from Defendant, Marc Dana Merker's 50% share of the net proceeds of sale." There shall be no argument included. (5) Identify the allegation(s) in the Complaint by page and line number corresponding to Plaintiff's request for a permanent injunction prohibiting Defendant from "encumbering, assigning, transferring or selling his interest in the Subject Property." There shall be no argument included. (6) Identify the portion of the Points and Authorities in the moving papers by page and line number addressing Plaintiff's request for a permanent injunction prohibiting Defendant from "encumbering, assigning, transferring or selling his interest in the Subject Property." There shall be no argument included. (7) Identify evidence submitted with the moving papers by the declarant's name, paragraph number and exhibit number corresponding to Plaintiff's request in the proposed interlocutory judgment prohibiting Defendant from "encumbering, assigning, transferring or selling his interest in the Subject Property." There shall be no argument included. Supplemental brief shall not exceed 5 pages and shall be limited to the issue delineated above. Supplemental brief shall be filed and served no later than January 16, 2023 by 5:00 p.m.=(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
